GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Valentino's Diary collection is set to transport you to the Italian seaside", "With many currently grounded, a little international escapism through our wardrobe is just the ticket.", "At a time when we are safely staying put and maintaining a low-key schedule, many can't help but feel a tiny loss for the days when jetting off to a seaside European locale was a more than reasonable way to spend a summer vacation. Given this, our wardrobes have become a substitute of sorts for international travel, the investment in beautifully crafted, brightly coloured pieces cut in floaty silhouettes transporting us overseas and casting our minds ahead to the unforgettable times we hope to soon spend on the warm sands of the Mediterranean. Designed as a reliable constant for our summer wardrobes no matter where you may find yourself, Valentino's Resort 2021 Diary collection is filled with warm weather-ready pieces we've already begun mentally packing into our currently-stowed away suitcases. Ideated under the notion that simplification is the best means for amplification, creative director Pierpaolo Piccioli ensured that the Roman house's latest resort offering relied on nothing other than simple shapes and elementary outlines to create versatile pieces that don't compromise on vision. Focusing simply on colour, texture and lines, each piece pulls focus from trends and narrows in on workmanship and single pieces with multiple possibilities. It's an approach that's harmonious with our collective efforts to create capsule wardrobes containing more forever pieces and less seasonality. Resolving the complexities that can come with assembling a look, and ridding the curating process of an excessive number of variables, each offering within Valentino's newest resort collection has been radically reduced to the core needs of clothing. With cut being a clear focus of the collection, elementary geometric shapes combined with floaty fabrics lend themselves to clothing that appears to be barely laid on the body, the perfect sartorial salve for summer heat. Paired with perfectly balanced proportions, pieces such as tunics, tops, trousers and capes come with an effortless feel and fluidity of movement. Created in a colour palette which brightly boasts ideal summer wear, solid shades of green, red, pink - as well as 18th-century tapestries re-imagined in a similarly vibrant colourway - only amplify the versatility and uncontrived feel of Piccioli's latest take on resort wear. And if you haven't yet been sold on the idea of simple silhouettes boasting vivid hues as the solution to all your summer wardrobe needs, perhaps the collection's campaign backdropped against the designer's native Nettuno will? The Italian seaside town making our visions of being beach-bound abundantly clearer. Departing ever so slightly from the collection's simplified feel is its accessories line. Parting from the reductionist approach adopted for resort 2021's ready-to-wear arm, the Valentino Garavani Accessories collection looks to long-loved house codes in the design. Veering away from a less dimensional feel, Valentino's newest accessories are extremely textured and tactile - think feminine bows, frills, and flower petals transposed onto summer staples including short-brimmed, generously-sized bags, and sandals. Naturally, Valentino's signature stud too appears in the house's resort 2021 collection via the new Valentino Garavani Roman Stud collection. Supersizing the stud to give a slightly punk feel to all which it adorns, the metallic motif appears on a number of pieces, including chain bags crafted out of nappa leather, calfskin sandals and ever-popular mule styles. Shop the Valentino Resort 2021 Diary collection, as well as the new Valentino Garavani Roman Stud collection in store by booking an appointment with Valentino boutique staff. For Valentino's Sydney Westfield boutique, call (61) 02 8404 0888 or email vau.swf@valentino.com, or, for the Melbourne Chadstone boutique, call (61) 03 9568 7559 or email vau.cha@valentino.com. You can also request an in store appointment here."
